### TypeScript: Parse Execution Output Line by Line

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.2.0/messaging/page.mdx

The `parseOutput` function is an asynchronous utility for processing an `Execution`. It iterates through lines of output, calling specific callback functions (`onStdout`, `onStderr`, `onResult`, `onError`) for different types of messages (stdout, stderr, results, or errors).

```typescript
function parseOutput(
   execution: Execution, 
   line: string, 
   onStdout?: (output: OutputMessage) => any, 
   onStderr?: (output: OutputMessage) => any, 
   onResult?: (data: Result) => any,
 onError?: (error: ExecutionError) => any): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### Stream stdout and stderr from code execution

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/streaming/page.mdx

Handle standard output and standard error streams from code executed in the E2B sandbox. This involves providing `onStdout`/`onStderr` (JavaScript) or `on_stdout`/`on_stderr` (Python) callbacks to capture and process the output lines, including runtime errors.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const codeToRun = `
  import time
  import sys
  print("This goes first to stdout")
  time.sleep(3)
  print("This goes later to stderr", file=sys.stderr)
  time.sleep(3)
  print("This goes last")
`
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
sandbox.runCode(codeToRun, {
  // Use `onError` to handle runtime code errors
  onError: error => console.error('error:', error), 
  onStdout: data => console.log('stdout:', data), 
  onStderr: data => console.error('stderr:', data), 
})
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

code_to_run = """
  import time
  import sys
  print("This goes first to stdout")
  time.sleep(3)
  print("This goes later to stderr", file=sys.stderr)
  time.sleep(3)
  print("This goes last")
"""

sandbox = Sandbox.create()
sandbox.run_code(
  code_to_run,
  # Use `on_error` to handle runtime code errors
  on_error=lambda error: print('error:', error), 
  on_stdout=lambda data: print('stdout:', data), 
  on_stderr=lambda data: print('stderr:', data), 
)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Stdout Output Callback - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/commands/page.mdx

Sets up an optional callback function to handle standard output from a command. The callback receives output as a string and can be asynchronous.

```typescript
optional onStdout: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Parse Execution Output

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.0.4/messaging/page.mdx

Parses the output of an execution, handling standard output, standard error, results, and errors using provided callback functions. This is essential for managing the lifecycle of a sandboxed process.

```typescript
/**
 * Parses the output of an execution, handling stdout, stderr, results, and errors.
 * @param {Execution} execution The execution context.
 * @param {string} line The current line of output to parse.
 * @param {(output: OutputMessage) => any} [onStdout] Callback for standard output messages.
 * @param {(output: OutputMessage) => any} [onStderr] Callback for standard error messages.
 * @param {(data: Result) => any} [onResult] Callback for result data.
 * @param {(error: ExecutionError) => any} [onError] Callback for execution errors.
 * @returns {Promise<void>} A promise that resolves when parsing is complete.
 */
function parseOutput(
   execution: Execution, 
   line: string, 
   onStdout?: (output: OutputMessage) => any, 
   onStderr?: (output: OutputMessage) => any, 
   onResult?: (data: Result) => any,
   onError?: (error: ExecutionError) => any): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Parse Execution Output

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.1.1/messaging/page.mdx

The `parseOutput` function asynchronously processes an `Execution` object, parsing its output line by line. It supports callbacks for handling standard output, standard error, results, and errors, facilitating real-time feedback during execution.

```typescript
function parseOutput(
   execution: Execution, 
   line: string, 
   onStdout?: (output: OutputMessage) => any, 
   onStderr?: (output: OutputMessage) => any, 
   onResult?: (data: Result) => any,
onError?: (error: ExecutionError) => any): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Stderr Output Callback - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/commands/page.mdx

Sets up an optional callback function to handle standard error output from a command. The callback receives output as a string and can be asynchronous.

```typescript
optional onStderr: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
```

--------------------------------

### Stream Command Output with Callbacks (JS/Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/commands/streaming/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to stream command output (stdout and stderr) in real-time during command execution. It utilizes `onStdout` and `onStderr` callbacks in JavaScript and `on_stdout` and `on_stderr` callbacks in Python, provided by the E2B code interpreter sandbox. The callbacks process data chunks as they are received, allowing for live output monitoring.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
const result = await sandbox.commands.run('echo hello; sleep 1; echo world', {
  onStdout: (data) => {
    console.log(data)
  },
  onStderr: (data) => {
    console.log(data)
  },
})
console.log(result)
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox.create()

result = sandbox.commands.run('echo hello; sleep 1; echo world', on_stdout=lambda data: print(data), on_stderr=lambda data: print(data))
print(result)
```

--------------------------------

### Command Start Options with Defaults - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.0/commands/page.mdx

Illustrates the default values for various options when starting a command. For instance, the 'cwd' defaults to the user's home directory, 'envs' defaults to an empty object, and 'user' defaults to the sandbox's user. Callbacks like 'onStderr' and 'onStdout' are optional.

```typescript
background?: boolean;
cwd?: string; // Default: // home directory of the user used to start the command
envs?: Record<string, string>; // Default: {}
onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
requestTimeoutMs?: number;
timeoutMs?: number;
user?: Username; // Default: user
```

--------------------------------

### Watch Directory for Filesystem Events (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/filesystem/page.mdx

The `watchDir` function allows you to monitor a specified directory for various filesystem events such as file creation, modification, or deletion. It takes a callback function that is executed for each event. The function returns a `WatchHandle` which can be used to stop the watching process. Optional `WatchOpts` can be provided for configuration.

```typescript
watchDir(
   path: string, 
   onEvent: (event: FilesystemEvent) => void | Promise<void>,
   opts?: WatchOpts
): Promise<WatchHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Watch Directory for Filesystem Events (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/filesystem/page.mdx

Starts watching a specified directory for filesystem events. It takes a path, a callback function to handle events, and optional watch options. The function returns a Promise that resolves to a WatchHandle, which can be used to stop watching the directory. Dependencies include FilesystemEvent and WatchOpts.

```TypeScript
watchDir(
   path: string, 
   onEvent: (event: FilesystemEvent) => void | Promise<void>,
opts?: WatchOpts): Promise<WatchHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Watch Directory for Events

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.12.0/filesystem/page.mdx

Starts watching a specified directory for filesystem events. It requires the directory path, a callback function to handle events, and optional watch options. The callback receives a FilesystemEvent and can be asynchronous. It returns a Promise resolving to a WatchHandle, which can be used to stop watching.

```typescript
watchDir(
   path: string, 
   onEvent: (event: FilesystemEvent) => void | Promise<void>,
opts?: WatchOpts & object): Promise<WatchHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Watch Directory for Filesystem Events (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/filesystem/page.mdx

Starts watching a specified directory for filesystem events. It takes a path, an event handler callback, and optional watch options. The function returns a `WatchHandle` that can be used to stop the watching process. The `onEvent` callback receives a `FilesystemEvent` object.

```typescript
watchDir(
   path: string,
   onEvent: (event: FilesystemEvent) => void | Promise<void>,
opts?: WatchOpts): Promise<WatchHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Command Start Options in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.3/commands/page.mdx

Defines the options for starting a new command in the E2B environment. This includes settings for running commands in the background, specifying the working directory (cwd), environment variables (envs), and callbacks for standard error (onStderr) and standard output (onStdout). It also supports request and command timeouts, and specifying the user to run the command as.

```ts
{
  background?: boolean;
  cwd?: string;
  envs?: Record<string, string>;
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Run Python Code

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.0.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Executes Python code within the E2B sandbox. This method supports various options for customization, including specifying a different language or context, and handling execution outcomes via callbacks like `onError`, `onResult`, `onStderr`, and `onStdout`. It also allows setting request and execution timeouts.

```typescript
runCode(code: string, opts?: RunCodeOpts & object): Promise<Execution>
```

--------------------------------

### Format Sandbox Timeout Error in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

A utility function to format and create a TimeoutError specifically for sandbox-related timeouts. It helps in standardizing how timeout errors are represented within the E2B SDK. It takes a string message and returns a TimeoutError object.

```typescript
function formatSandboxTimeoutError(message: string): TimeoutError {
  return new TimeoutError(message);
}
```


### Watch Directory for Events (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.1/filesystem/page.mdx

Starts watching a specified directory for filesystem events (e.g., file creation, deletion, modification). It takes a callback function to handle events and returns a WatchHandle for managing the watch.

```typescript
const watchHandle = await filesystem.watchDir('/path/to/directory', (event) => {
  console.log('Filesystem event:', event);
});

// To stop watching later:
// await watchHandle.unwatch();
```

### Watch Directory for Filesystem Events in E2B

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/filesystem/page.mdx

The watchDir function enables monitoring a directory for filesystem events. It requires a path to the directory and a callback function to handle events. It returns a WatchHandle for stopping the watch operation. Optional connection and watch options can be provided.

```typescript
watchDir(
   path: string, 
   onEvent: (event: FilesystemEvent) => void | Promise<void>, 
opts?: WatchOpts & object): Promise<WatchHandle>
```

### Define InvalidArgumentError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/errors/page.mdx

The InvalidArgumentError is thrown when a function or method receives arguments that do not meet the expected criteria. It can optionally accept a stack trace string for debugging purposes. This error is crucial for maintaining data integrity within the SDK.

```typescript
class InvalidArgumentError {
  constructor(message: string, stackTrace?: string)
}
```

```typescript
  // ... implementation
```

### Execute Code in E2B Sandbox (JavaScript/TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/README.md

Demonstrates how to create a sandbox instance, run code within it, and retrieve the execution results using the E2B JavaScript SDK. It requires the @e2b/code-interpreter package.

```typescript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
await sandbox.runCode('x = 1')

const execution = await sandbox.runCode('x+=1; x')
console.log(execution.text)  // outputs 2
```

```typescript

function parseOutput(
```

### TypeScript Optional Timeout Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/filesystem/page.mdx

Defines an optional timeout in milliseconds for watch operations. A value of 0 disables the timeout. The default timeout is 60 seconds.

```typescript
// Default: 60_000 // 60 seconds
```

### Define InvalidArgumentError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.0/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when an invalid argument is provided to a function or method. This error inherits from SandboxError.

```typescript
class InvalidArgumentError extends SandboxError {
  message: string;

  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.message = message;
  }
}
```

### Run commands in background (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/commands/background/page.mdx

Execute a command in the background using the `commands.run()` method with the `background: true` option. The command continues to run after the method returns. It can be terminated later using `command.kill()`. The `onStdout` callback receives data as it's produced.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()

// Start the command in the background
const command = await sandbox.commands.run('echo hello; sleep 10; echo world', {
  background: true, // $HighlightLine
  onStdout: (data) => {
    console.log(data)
  },
})

// Kill the command
await command.kill()
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: TimeoutError Constructor

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.5/errors/page.mdx

Represents various timeout scenarios, including sandbox timeouts, request timeouts, and command execution timeouts. It takes a string message.

```typescript
new TimeoutError(message: string): TimeoutError
```

### Watch Directory Events in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.3/filesystem/page.mdx

This function allows you to monitor a specified directory for any filesystem events. It takes the directory path and a callback function to handle events. Optional connection options can also be provided. The function returns a handle to stop watching.

```typescript
import { watch, WatchOpts } from 'e2b';

async function example() {
  const watchHandle = await watch('/path/to/directory', (event) => {
    console.log('File event:', event);
  }, {
    timeoutMs: 60_000,
    requestTimeoutMs: 30_000,
    user: 'user1'
  } as WatchOpts);

  // To stop watching:
  // await watchHandle.stop();
}
```
